title: The Camel
weakest_section: 17:00-18:00 range with Andy's shoe shine aftermath

jokes.text: We've had someone throw acid at it, tomato sauce. Someone tried to stab it once. We really need better security here. We also need... better, less-offensive history.
jokes.setup: Explaining the mural has been vandalized
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions stabbing, then pivots to needing 'less-offensive history' as if that's the real solution
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from minor vandalism to stabbing, with perfect deadpan delivery. The 'less-offensive history' line is brilliant misdirection that shows Leslie's optimistic problem-solving mindset.

jokes.text: Leslie, what's your design gonna be? A tree? / Joe, you work in sewage. Your department literally specializes in crap.
jokes.setup: Joe dismissively asks if Leslie will design a tree
jokes.punchline: Leslie fires back that his department specializes in crap
jokes.explanation: Solid comeback with good structure. Shows Leslie's competitive nature but relies on obvious wordplay.

jokes.text: Crap is a slang term, and I don't like that term. But at least we don't specialize in losing... Like you guys.
jokes.setup: Joe corrects the terminology about his department
jokes.punchline: Immediately fires back with 'specializing in losing'
jokes.explanation: Good comeback structure with the fake-out correction leading to the real insult. Character-appropriate pettiness.

jokes.text: How does sewage always get the hottest interns?
jokes.setup: Sewage department employees are leaving
jokes.punchline: Tom notices they somehow attract attractive interns despite working with sewage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom Haverford observation - shallow and petty but delivered with genuine confusion. The absurdity of attractive people choosing sewage work is great.

jokes.text: Look at Madonna... Great singer, amazing arms. Look at O.J. Simpson... Heisman trophy winner, naked gun.
jokes.setup: Leslie gives examples of people with multiple legacies
jokes.punchline: Completely ignores O.J.'s murder trial, mentions the Naked Gun movies instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Leslie's relentless optimism and ability to see the best in everything, even O.J. Simpson. The misdirection from obvious to obscure legacy is perfect.

jokes.text: That's not true. I've seen your fingernails. / I pay someone to do this.
jokes.setup: Leslie compliments Donna's nail art skills
jokes.punchline: Donna reveals she pays for professional manicures
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Donna's lifestyle and Leslie's earnest mistake. Well-structured but predictable.

jokes.text: Only Ron can order the whole department to do something. Ron, order them to do this. / Do whatever Leslie says.
jokes.setup: Leslie realizes she doesn't have authority to order people around
jokes.punchline: Ron immediately and minimally complies, giving Leslie exactly what she wants
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson moment - minimal words, maximum effect. Shows the Leslie/Ron dynamic beautifully.

jokes.text: Designers, make it work. / Tim Gunn.
jokes.setup: Leslie channels Project Runway with 'make it work'
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately identifies the reference
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference that works for both characters - Leslie's earnest borrowing and Tom's media awareness.

jokes.text: I got my first job when I was nine. Worked at a sheet metal factory. In two weeks, I was running the floor. Child labor laws are ruining this country.
jokes.setup: Ron talks about his early work experience
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurd claim about running a factory at nine, then blames child labor laws
jokes.explanation: Peak Ron Swanson - absurd backstory delivered deadpan, escalating to the perfect libertarian punchline. Quintessential character writing.

jokes.text: No, I have a bunion that's practically its own toe. Normally, the pain howls through my loafers like a banshee on the moors, but... for these past three minutes, it's been reduced to a faint growl.
jokes.setup: Jerry explains why his foot feels different
jokes.punchline: Incredibly elaborate and poetic description of bunion pain relief
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jerry moment - overly elaborate language for mundane medical condition. The 'banshee on the moors' imagery is wonderfully pretentious.

jokes.text: Here. Just gimme $20 worth of art. Just something that seems personal that only I could have done. Well, tell me about yourself. No. Just paint.
jokes.setup: Tom wants personal art but won't share anything personal
jokes.punchline: Refuses to provide any information for the artist to work with
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom contradiction - wants something personal and unique while providing zero input. Shows his surface-level understanding of art.

jokes.text: I have no interest in art. Let me clarify. I have no interest in non-nude images.
jokes.setup: Ron states he has no interest in art
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he only likes nude art
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron delivery - the 'clarification' format makes it sound reasonable while revealing his singular focus. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Dude, what the hell kind of art is this? Looks like a lizard puking up skittles! / I'm an abstract expressionist. / No, you're a con artist, and I'm a guy that's out 20 bucks.
jokes.setup: Tom criticizes the abstract art he commissioned
jokes.punchline: Artist claims to be abstract expressionist, Tom calls him a con artist
jokes.explanation: Good setup and wordplay with 'abstract expressionist' vs 'con artist.' The 'lizard puking skittles' description is vivid and funny.

jokes.text: I know everything about this town and these murals, and that's why this is a dream come true... Literally. I have had a dream where I designed a mural. But then it turned into a nightmare because the mural started talking, and it came alive, it was whispering, and I couldn't hear what it was saying, so I leaned in close, and then it ate me. At one point, Gina Gershon was there.
jokes.setup: Leslie explains this is literally a dream come true
jokes.punchline: Escalates into increasingly bizarre nightmare scenario ending with random Gina Gershon appearance
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie over-sharing with perfect escalation to absurdity. The Gina Gershon detail is wonderfully random and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Never understood the term elbow grease. I guess it's not really grease. Just hard work.
jokes.setup: Andy contemplates the phrase 'elbow grease'
jokes.punchline: Takes the idiom literally and works through the logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - taking common phrases literally with genuine confusion. Shows his childlike thought process.

jokes.text: I feel right at home as a shoeshine. I have no idea what I'm doing, but I know I'm doing it really, really well.
jokes.setup: Andy reflects on his shoeshine work
jokes.punchline: Claims to have no idea what he's doing but knows he's great at it
jokes.explanation: Beautiful Andy logic - complete contradiction delivered with total confidence. Captures his delusional self-assurance perfectly.

jokes.text: That looks like something a death row convict would make in art therapy.
jokes.setup: Ann shows her collage design
jokes.punchline: Tom's brutal but specific critique
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and mean insult that perfectly captures Tom's fashion sense applied to art criticism. The specificity makes it memorable.

jokes.text: You can't make art because you are art. You're beautiful... But that sucks.
jokes.setup: Tom tries to console Ann with a compliment
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts the sweet sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment - tries to be smooth and sweet but can't help being honest about her bad art. Great structure with the immediate undercut.

jokes.text: A piece of art caused me to have an emotional reaction. Is that normal?
jokes.setup: Andy stares at Tom's abstract shapes
jokes.punchline: Confused by having an emotional response to art
jokes.explanation: Sweet Andy moment showing his innocence about cultural experiences. The genuine confusion about art appreciation is endearing.

jokes.text: Who's the Jesus? / That would be Greg Kinnear. / I didn't know he was from Indiana. / Yeah, you know, I read that he was.
jokes.setup: Jerry recreates the Last Supper with Indiana celebrities
jokes.punchline: Chooses Greg Kinnear as Jesus with flimsy justification
jokes.explanation: Absurd choice for Jesus with Jerry's characteristic vague reasoning. The mundane celebrity choice for religious figure is funny.

jokes.text: He was great on ER... / Greg Kinnear wasn't in ER. / Yeah, he was. / I don't think that he was. / Who am I thinking of?
jokes.setup: Jerry tries to justify Greg Kinnear choice
jokes.punchline: Gets confused about what shows Kinnear was in
jokes.explanation: Typical Jerry confusion and mistake. Character-appropriate but not particularly memorable or well-structured.

jokes.text: You said 'murinal'. / No, I didn't. / You said 'murinal'. I heard it. / Why don't you put that murinal in the men's room so people can murinate all over it?
jokes.setup: Tom catches Jerry saying 'murinal' instead of 'mural'
jokes.punchline: Escalates with bathroom-themed puns
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay escalation but relies on bathroom humor. Shows Tom's petty nature in seizing on Jerry's mistake.

jokes.text: Go to the doctor. You might have a murinary tract infection. / Murinal, murinal, murinal!
jokes.setup: Continuing the 'murinal' joke
jokes.punchline: Medical pun followed by chanting
jokes.explanation: Continues the pun but feels forced. The chanting shows Tom's immaturity but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: It's pointillism. And each dot is a photo of a citizen of the town. / No one cares. At all.
jokes.setup: Jerry explains his elaborate artistic technique
jokes.punchline: Tom's brutal dismissal
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Jerry's earnest artistic effort and Tom's complete dismissal. The bluntness is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: This is a human-sized hamster wheel that will be next to the mural, if we can get one, and it'll be spinning, and there'll be like a fat guy in it all the time like screaming and like eating raw beef and like bleeding, and like blood will come out of his mouth and stuff.
jokes.setup: April describes her multimedia art project
jokes.punchline: Escalates to graphic description of suffering person in hamster wheel
jokes.explanation: Peak April darkness and absurdity. Perfectly captures her goth sensibility and desire to disturb people. The escalation is intentionally over-the-top.

jokes.text: I have one question. Why? / If you have to ask, you don't get it.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks for explanation of April's disturbing art
jokes.punchline: April gives pretentious artist response
jokes.explanation: Perfect April response using pretentious art world clichés to defend her deliberately disturbing vision.

jokes.text: Like the Mona Lisa or the music of Squeeze.
jokes.setup: Leslie talks about things that stand the test of time
jokes.punchline: Compares the Mona Lisa to the band Squeeze
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment showing her earnest but questionable cultural knowledge. The mismatch between high art and 80s pop is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Look at the oscars. This is our holocaust movie. This is our English Patient.
jokes.setup: Leslie explains why tragic art wins awards
jokes.punchline: Compares local bread factory fire to Holocaust movies
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's competitive mindset and understanding of award dynamics, but the comparison is appropriately tone-deaf for her character.

jokes.text: One to one to one to one to one to one. / We all voted for ourselves, didn't we?
jokes.setup: Tom reads the voting results
jokes.punchline: Leslie realizes everyone voted for their own design
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the team-building exercise showing human nature. Well-structured reveal with Leslie's dawning realization.

jokes.text: What the ****, man? / I don't know what happened. Frankly. I emitted a noise. The noise was involuntary. Sometimes a sound is just a sound, you know?
jokes.setup: Customer is upset about something Andy did while shining shoes
jokes.punchline: Andy's awkward explanation about making an involuntary noise
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment with Andy's characteristic awkward over-explanation. The vague nature makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Ann's blows. / Don't hold back.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks if they can eliminate any designs
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately targets Ann, she responds sarcastically
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective. Tom's bluntness and Ann's dry response work well together.

jokes.text: Only god could do that!
jokes.setup: Tom says any kid could make those shapes
jokes.punchline: Andy escalates to divine intervention
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Andy's continued attachment to the shapes. Goes from personal to religious significance.

jokes.text: It's like if you got Michelangelo and Andy Warhol and Jackson Pollock and Jim Davis from Garfield to do one painting. Imagine how good that painting would be!
jokes.setup: Leslie explains their collaborative approach
jokes.punchline: Includes Jim Davis (Garfield creator) with legendary artists
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment showing her earnest but misguided cultural knowledge. The escalation from masters to cartoon creator is brilliantly delivered.

jokes.text: Ann's in trouble. We think it might be pills. / What? / That's a lie. But this is as important.
jokes.setup: Leslie claims Ann is in drug trouble
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits it's a lie but insists this is equally important
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie manipulation showing her priorities and willingness to lie for work. The immediate confession makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You made a camel. You've never heard that saying? A camel is actually a horse designed by a committee
jokes.setup: Mark looks at their combined artwork
jokes.punchline: Explains the 'designed by committee' saying
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on their collaborative process. The saying perfectly describes what happened to their mural.

jokes.text: I've seen you sketch things. Like poles for stop signs. That everybody stops and looks at. / By law, they're required to.
jokes.setup: Leslie compliments Mark's artistic ability
jokes.punchline: Mark deflates the compliment with literal truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Leslie's earnest compliment. Shows both her optimism and Mark's pragmatic nature.

jokes.text: I was gonna go to Arby's and watch Frontline.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks what Mark was going to do tonight
jokes.punchline: Combines fast food with serious documentary watching
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail showing Mark's practical but cultured lifestyle. The combination is unexpectedly specific and funny.

jokes.text: It's like what motels put up, and it hangs there for years, and no one ever throws acid at it.
jokes.setup: Mark explains why his boring art will win
jokes.punchline: References the acid attacks from the opening
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the opening with practical wisdom about safe art choices. Shows Mark's understanding of public taste.

jokes.text: I'd take Jerry's Murinal over this.
jokes.setup: Tom criticizing Mark's art
jokes.punchline: Uses Jerry's mispronunciation as an insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Tom's pettiness and inability to let go of jokes. Brings back the wordplay effectively.

jokes.text: How's life in the Parks De-fart-ment? / Better than life in the Sewage De-fart-ment. Which makes more sense.
jokes.setup: Joe makes juvenile department name joke
jokes.punchline: Leslie turns it back on him with better logic
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor but Leslie's comeback has good logic. Shows the ongoing department rivalry.

jokes.text: The moan. The weird moan you made that was super-weird? Do you not remember that? I talked about it with the lady who went after for 1/2 hour. She said she thought it was an animal...
jokes.setup: Customer explains what Andy did wrong
jokes.punchline: Reveals Andy made animal-like noises during shoe shining
jokes.explanation: Good callback that explains the earlier incident. The detail about discussing it with another customer makes it more embarrassing.

jokes.text: It's your basic dogs playing poker, but, with an everything's-on-fire theme.
jokes.setup: Police Chief presents their department's mural concept
jokes.punchline: Combines classic kitsch art with apocalyptic theme
jokes.explanation: Great combination of classic bad art with random dark twist. The casual way it's presented makes it funnier.

jokes.text: This is my nephew over here. And this is an attractive lady with a hamburger for a head.
jokes.setup: Chief continues describing their mural
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions hamburger-headed woman
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd detail delivered matter-of-factly. The specificity of 'attractive lady with hamburger head' is perfectly random.

jokes.text: It looks like he's carrying Kinnear into the burning building. / That's because he's moonwalking. So he... Should be goin' the other way.
jokes.setup: Ann observes Michael Jackson appears to be carrying Jesus into danger
jokes.punchline: Jerry realizes the moonwalk direction makes it look wrong
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy concept with Jerry's dawning realization of the artistic error. The moonwalk logistics create unintended narrative.

jokes.text: They're changing the title to the diversity express.
jokes.setup: Leslie explains they're keeping the old controversial mural
jokes.punchline: Reveals the absurd new 'politically correct' name
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of superficial solutions to deeper problems. The name change instead of actual change is spot-on bureaucracy humor.

jokes.text: Shut up and do more art for me.
jokes.setup: Artist complains about having actual school assignments
jokes.punchline: Tom dismisses real responsibilities for his whims
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom entitlement and dismissal of others' priorities. The bluntness is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I like the green one and the red circle right here. / I'm tearing up, man.
jokes.setup: Andy continues studying the abstract shapes
jokes.punchline: Gets emotional over basic geometric forms
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback showing Andy's continued genuine emotional connection to simple art. His sincerity makes it endearing rather than mocking.

tmdb_overview: After one of the murals ("The Spirit of Pawnee") in City Hall is defaced, Leslie and the Parks Department compete to come up with a new design. Meanwhile, Andy and Ron share an uncomfortable moment at the shoeshine stand.
insight: Absurdist energy peaks at 2.24 jokes per minute across 47 gags in Season 2's tightest hour.
